description:
You are a web-developer who has been handed two raw Apache access-log snapshots, located at:

• /home/user/project/logs/day1_access.log  
• /home/user/project/logs/day2_access.log  

Each file already exists and contains exactly 5 log-entries, one per line, in the classical “combined” format, e.g.

IP – – [timestamp] "METHOD PATH HTTP/VERSION" STATUS BYTES "REFERER" "USER-AGENT"

The company security team needs a quick, line-aligned comparison that shows:

1. the client IP address (field 1, space-delimited) coming from day1_access.log  
2. the requested path/endpoint (field 7, space-delimited) coming from day1_access.log  
3. the HTTP status code (field 9, space-delimited) coming from day2_access.log (same row number)

You may NOT re-type or manually edit the log entries.  
Instead, produce the report purely with standard shell utilities that specialise in “cutting” columns out of a file and then “pasting” them together side-by-side.

Final deliverables
==================
1. /home/user/project/reports/combined_report.csv  
   • Directory “reports” must be created if it does not exist.  
   • File must contain exactly 5 lines (matching the number of log rows).  
   • Format of every line: 

        <ip>,<endpoint>,<status>

     ‑ The three fields are separated by a single literal comma “,”.  
     ‑ There are NO surrounding spaces.  
     ‑ Every line ends with a single Unix newline (LF).  
     ‑ Example pattern (do not copy verbatim):  

        127.0.0.1,/index.html,200

2. /home/user/project/reports/command_log.txt  
   • Append, in chronological order, every command you actually executed in the terminal to create the CSV.  
   • One command per line, no extra commentary.  
   • This file is used only for automated grading to ensure the solution employed “cut” and “paste” (both words must appear at least once).  

Important notes
---------------  
• Use only utilities available in a standard GNU/Linux userland (bash coreutils).  
• Do not assume root privileges.  
• The original log files must remain unchanged.  
• The grader will fail if the CSV contains extra whitespace, missing/extra lines, wrong order, or if command_log.txt omits commands.  
• Work strictly inside /home/user; do not create files elsewhere.  
• Remember: the goal is to demonstrate proper use of “cut” and “paste” for column manipulation, so structure your workflow accordingly.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts)
------------------------------------------------------
Directory: /home/user/project/logs

File: /home/user/project/logs/day1_access.log  
Exact contents (5 lines, LF line endings):
192.168.1.10 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:00:00 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1043 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
172.16.0.3 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:02:47 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 560 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
93.184.216.34 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:03:12 +0000] "GET /images/logo.png HTTP/1.1" 304 0 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.0.2.44 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:05:55 +0000] "GET /about HTTP/1.1" 200 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:07:23 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

File: /home/user/project/logs/day2_access.log  
Exact contents (5 lines, LF line endings):
192.168.1.10 - - [16/Sep/2023:10:00:00 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1100 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
172.16.0.3 - - [16/Sep/2023:10:02:47 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 200 580 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
93.184.216.34 - - [16/Sep/2023:10:03:12 +0000] "GET /images/logo.png HTTP/1.1" 304 0 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.0.2.44 - - [16/Sep/2023:10:05:55 +0000] "GET /about HTTP/1.1" 503 2100 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [16/Sep/2023:10:07:23 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 200 540 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

Desired final state
-------------------
Directory /home/user/project/reports must exist.

File: /home/user/project/reports/combined_report.csv  
Exact required contents (5 lines, LF line endings, no trailing blank line):
192.168.1.10,/index.html,200
172.16.0.3,/login,200
93.184.216.34,/images/logo.png,304
192.0.2.44,/about,503
203.0.113.5,/contact,200

File: /home/user/project/reports/command_log.txt  
• Must exist.  
• At least two separate lines, each a shell command executed by the agent.  
• At least one of the lines must contain the word “cut” and at least one line must contain the word “paste”.  
• The grader does NOT verify exact commands, only that they are present and non-empty.

Permissions
-----------
All new files and directories should be created with default user writable permissions (umask 002 or stricter).  
No root privileges are required or expected.
